from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about a written work into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Group triples by their subject
    subjects: dict[str, list[Triple]] = {}
    for t in triples:
        subjects.setdefault(t.subject, []).append(t)

    # Helper to turn a predicate/object into a clause fragment
    def phrase_for(pred: str, obj: str) -> str:
        mapping = {
            "author": f"written by {obj}",
            "isbnNumber": f"has ISBN {obj}",
            "language": f"is in {obj}",
            "mediaType": f"is a {obj}",
            "numberOfPages": f"has {obj} pages",
            "followedBy": f"is followed by {obj}",
            "precededBy": f"is preceded by {obj}",
            "publisher": f"published by {obj}",
            "publicationYear": f"was published in {obj}",
            "edition": f"is the {obj} edition",
            "genre": f"is a {obj} work",
            "literaryGenre": f"is a {obj} work",
            "releaseDate": f"was released on {obj}",
            "birthDate": f"was born on {obj}",
            "deathPlace": f"died in {obj}",
            "birthPlace": f"was born in {obj}",
            "capital": f"has capital {obj}",
            "country": f"is from {obj}",
            "location": f"is located in {obj}",
            "largestCity": f"has its largest city {obj}",
            "headquarter": f"is headquartered in {obj}",
        }
        return mapping.get(pred, f"{pred} {obj}")

    sentences = []
    for subj, triples_list in subjects.items():
        # Start with the subject name
        clause_parts = []
        for t in triples_list:
            frag = phrase_for(t.predicate, t.object)
            clause_parts.append(frag)

        # Combine fragments: first fragment as main clause, rest as relative clauses
        if clause_parts:
            main = clause_parts[0]
            if len(clause_parts) == 1:
                sentence = f"{subj} {main}"
            else:
                rel = ", ".join(clause_parts[1:])
                sentence = f"{subj} {main}, which {rel}"
            sentences.append(sentence)

    # Join multiple subject sentences with a coordinating conjunction
    if not sentences:
        return ""
    if len(sentences) == 1:
        return sentences[0] + "."
    else:
        return "; ".join(sentences) + "."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

      """
      Convert a list of triples about a written work into a single fluent sentence.
      """
      if not triples:
          return ""
  
      parts = []
      for t in triples:
          pred = t.predicate
          # Simple natural‑language mapping for common predicates
          if pred == "author":
              phrase = f"{t.subject} was written by {t.object}"
          elif pred == "isbnNumber":
              phrase = f"{t.subject} has ISBN {t.object}"
          elif pred == "language":
              phrase = f"{t.subject} is in {t.object}"

              phrase = f"{t.subject} has {t.object} pages"
          elif pred == "followedBy":
              phrase = f"{t.subject} is followed by {t.object}"
          elif pred == "precededBy":
              phrase = f"{t.subject} is preceded by {t.object}"
          else:
              # Generic fallback
              phrase = f"{t.subject} {pred} {t.object}"
          parts.append(phrase)
